title: AGENTS.md
text: working conventions AI coding agents repository live CLAUDE.md. Open file first — covers package identity, layering, memory model, testing parallelism, code style, files must updated together, read-gh CLI constraint, pre-commit setup. file exists agents follow AGENTS.md convention discover conventions without know look CLAUDE.md specifically.

what: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
title: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
text: file durable contract Claude (AI coding agent) pdfium R package. Read first edit fresh worktree. Update discover convention worth recording.

title: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
text: Package name: pdfium (r prefix; matches magick/xml2/httr). Repository name: rpdfium GitHub. License: MIT (package) + BSD-3-Clause (bundled PDFium binary). CRAN target: v0.1.0 ships CRAN. Every change preserves CRAN-cleanliness.

title: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
text: Public functions snake_case prefixed pdf_*. Internal Rcpp helpers prefixed cpp_* live src/. Never re-export . R wrappers validate inputs error messages. C++ layer assumes arguments already validated; raises Rcpp::stop invariants shouldn’t normally trip.
code: R user → R API (R/) → Rcpp glue (src/*.cpp) → PDFium C ABI → libpdfium.{so|dylib|dll}

what: Memory model — the rule that bites if you forget it
title: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
text: Every PDFium handle (FPDF_DOCUMENT, FPDF_PAGE, etc.) lives behind R externalptr C finalizer registered via R_RegisterCFinalizerEx(..., TRUE). finalizer path calls FPDF_*Close*. runs, calls R_ClearExternalPtr pointer reads NULL subsequent access. makes pdf_close() safely idempotent. Children (pages, page objects) hold R-level reference parent (doc, page) GC can’t reclaim parent child. Always set parent prot slot child’s externalptr. Automatic close GC works — see vignettes/architecture.Rmd. large documents platform-sensitive code (Windows file-handle blocking deletion), call pdf_close() explicitly.

what: Testing — must be safe under parallel execution
title: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
text: DESCRIPTION sets Config/testthat/parallel: true. Every test-*.R runs subprocess. Never assume specific test-file order. Never share mutable state across files. Use withr::local_* / withr::defer() cleanup. Helpers go tests/testthat/helper-*.R (sourced every worker). Setup ’s truly -per-worker goes tests/testthat/setup.R. Fixtures load fixture_path("name") (see helper-fixtures.R). Never embed hardcoded path. valgrind ASan runs override Config/testthat/parallel false — don’t write tests require parallelism pass.

title: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
text: R: tidyverse style enforced lintr styler. Run pre-commit run ---files pushing. C++: C++17, exceptions across C ABI boundary (translate Rcpp::stop). Header--source order, #include "fpdfview.h" first. Never write <<-. Never write assign(..., envir = .GlobalEnv). Comments: isn’t obvious. Don’t restate code says. Don’t reference PR numbers “added X flow” — belongs commit messages.

title: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
text: Never push main. Open PR feature branch. Branch naming: claude/<short-topic> AI-authored branches, feature/<topic> human-authored. Commit messages follow Conventional Commits: feat:, fix:, docs:, chore:, test:, refactor:, perf:, ci:, build:, style:, revert:. gh CLI read-machine. run gh pr create, gh pr edit, gh pr merge, gh issue create, gh issue comment, gh release create, gh api call non-GET method. pushing, give user suggested title body can open PR .

title: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
text: Install per fresh clone worktree first commit: pre-commit stage runs fast checks (lint, parsable, format). pre-push stage adds full lint, roxygen regen, parallel tests. CI re-runs full configuration missed local install still gets caught — local installation gives fastest feedback.
code: pip install --user pre-commit pre-commit install pre-commit install --hook-type pre-push

title: CLAUDE.md — Working conventions for AI contributors
text: Read vignettes/architecture.Rmd four-layer model memory-model contract. Read ADRs dev/decisions/. record every intentional choice — supersede new ADR, don’t edit accepted ones. plan file /home/bill/.claude/plans/pdfium-r-package-peaceful-frog.md captures full project roadmap.

title: Contributing to pdfium
text: Thanks interest contributing.

title: Contributing to pdfium
code: git clone https://github.com/billdenney/rpdfium cd rpdfium  # Install Python pre-commit (one-time, per clone) pip install --user pre-commit pre-commit install pre-commit install --hook-type pre-push  # Install R dev dependencies Rscript -e 'install.packages(c("devtools","testthat","covr","lintr","styler","withr","roxygen2"))'  # Configure the package (downloads the bblanchon PDFium binary) R CMD INSTALL .  # Run the test suite (parallel, per worker) Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'

title: Contributing to pdfium
text: Create feature branch (claude/... AI-authored, feature/... otherwise) main. Never push main directly. Make change. Add update tests every behavioural change. Run pre-commit run ---files passes. Run devtools::check() (R CMD check ---cran .) confirm zero errors / zero warnings. Run covr::package_coverage() confirm R coverage stays 100%. Untestable code paths must marked # nocov start / # nocov end comment explaining . Push branch open pull request using template .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md.

title: Contributing to pdfium
text: follow Conventional Commits: Allowed types: feat, fix, docs, chore, test, refactor, perf, ci, build, style, revert. Subject line 70 chars.
code: feat: short summary in imperative mood  Optional longer body. Reference issues with "Closes #N".

title: Contributing to pdfium
text: Material decisions (API shape, dependencies, binary distribution, memory model) recorded Architecture Decision Records dev/decisions/. Use template dev/decisions/ADR-000-template.md. ADRs immutable accepted — supersede new ADR references old.

title: Contributing to pdfium
text: .pre-commit-config.yaml splits hooks two stages: pre-commit runs every git commit. Fast checks: lintr, styler, parsable-R, spell-check, header sanity. Optimized tight feedback. pre-push runs git push. Slower checks: roxygen regeneration, full lint, parallel testthat run. Slow enough don’t want every commit cheap enough run branch leaves machine. pre-push hook fails, fix issue create new commit — don’t --amend (complicates code review).

title: Contributing to pdfium
text: pdfium targets CRAN v0.1.0 stays CRAN-clean. merging: R CMD check ---cran green. new internet calls happen R CMD check (install-time configure). Examples run 5 seconds . new \dontrun{} justified PR description.

title: How pdfium is built
text: R API takes user inputs, validates , calls Rcpp glue, shapes result back idiomatic R (tibbles tabular outputs, S3 objects handles). ever interact R API.
code: R user   │   ▼ R API           pdf_open(), pdf_close(), pdf_page_count(), ...   │             S3 classes: pdfium_doc, pdfium_page, pdfium_obj   ▼ Rcpp glue       internal cpp_* helpers; you should never see them   │   ▼ PDFium C ABI    Google's PDFium engine (BSD-3-Clause)   │   ▼ libpdfium       shared library downloaded at install time

what: Memory: what happens when you forget to close
title: How pdfium is built
text: safe. pdfium registers C finalizer every PDF handle. R object becomes unreachable, R’s garbage collector reclaims ; finalizer calls PDFium’s FPDF_CloseDocument release underlying memory. caveat GC eventual, deterministic. open many large documents tight loop, may exhaust process memory GC catches . case, close explicitly: pdf_close() idempotent: calling twice -op. finalizer notices handle already closed skips close call. can safely combine explicit close automatic fallback.
code: inspect <- function(path) {   doc <- pdf_open(path)   pdf_page_count(doc)   # `doc` goes out of scope here.   # R's GC will eventually finalize it and call FPDF_CloseDocument. } inspect("report.pdf") inspect <- function(path) {   doc <- pdf_open(path)   on.exit(pdf_close(doc), add = TRUE)   pdf_page_count(doc) }

title: How pdfium is built
text: call pdf_load_page(doc, ...), returned pdfium_page holds internal reference parent pdfium_doc. Even drop reference doc (explicitly close ), page stays valid page object collected. underlying PDFium document kept alive background last page (object) depends goes away. order : child references parent, never way around.
code: load_page <- function(path) {   doc <- pdf_open(path)   page <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   # available in Phase 1+   pdf_close(doc)                  # this is fine   page                            # still usable here }

title: How pdfium is built
text: library(pdfium), happens: R loads package’s compiled pdfium.(pdfium.dll Windows). dynamic linker follows RPATH baked install time loads libpdfium.{|dylib|dll} package’s inst/lib/. .onLoad calls FPDF_InitLibraryWithConfig() exactly . call library.unload("pdfium") quit R, .onUnload runs FPDF_DestroyLibrary(). libpdfium binary downloaded first time installed pdfium. pinned release tag lives tools/pdfium-version.txt. machine internet access install time, set PDFIUM_OFFLINE=1 place matching tarball inst/pdfium-binaries/ installing.

title: How pdfium is built
text: Holding many documents . GC non-deterministic; close explicitly pdf_close(). Deleting open PDF Windows. Windows blocks deletion files held open handles. Close document first, delete. Using closed handle. Functions take pdfium_doc raise error handle already closed. Re-open file need . Calling FPDF_* directly. Don’t. R API exists reason — bypassing bypasses lifetime tracking ’ll crash R.

title: How pdfium is built
text: decision records dev/decisions/ capture every architectural choice alternatives considered. dev/architecture.md covers contributor-facing topics: CI topology, PDFium bump procedure, fixture-rebuild pipeline. vignette("getting-started") walks complete inspection workflow.

what: Idiomatic R Bindings to the PDFium PDF Engine
title: Idiomatic R Bindings to the PDFium PDF Engine
text: pdfium provides idiomatic R bindings Google’s PDFium engine — library powers Chrome’s PDF viewer. complements pdftools qpdf exposing vector path geometry — stroke / fill / Bezier control points / transformation matrices — CRAN package surfaces today.

title: Idiomatic R Bindings to the PDFium PDF Engine
text: Auditing PDF figures (lines, colors, fonts). Extracting curves regulatory filings scientific publications. Building PDF normalization pipelines need geometry, just text. Anything ’d otherwise drop Python pypdfium2.

title: Idiomatic R Bindings to the PDFium PDF Engine
text: Pre-release. 0.1.0 CRAN target documented dev/decisions/ project plan. current public API documented pkgdown exercised 100% R coverage.

title: Close a PDF document — pdf_close
text: Releases underlying PDFium handle. Idempotent — calling pdf_close() already-closed document -op. finalizer registered pdf_open() also calls R object garbage-collected, explicit close recommended handling many large documents subsequent operation needs delete source file (relevant Windows).

title: Close a PDF document — pdf_close
code: pdf_close(doc)

title: Close a PDF document — pdf_close
text: doc pdfium_doc produced pdf_open().

title: Close a PDF document — pdf_close
text: Invisibly returns doc underlying pointer marked closed.

title: Close a page handle — pdf_close_page
text: Releases underlying PDFium handle. Idempotent — calling pdf_close_page() already-closed page -op.

title: Close a page handle — pdf_close_page
code: pdf_close_page(page)

title: Close a page handle — pdf_close_page
text: page pdfium_page pdf_load_page().

title: Close a page handle — pdf_close_page
text: Invisibly returns page underlying pointer marked closed.

what: Extract all path geometry on a page into a single tibble — pdf_extract_paths
title: Extract all path geometry on a page into a single tibble — pdf_extract_paths
text: One-call helper opens document (accepts already-open one), enumerates every path object requested page, returns tibble one row per path segment carrying geometry containing path's stroke / fill style bounding box. function kmextract consumes via pdfium_native backend.

title: Extract all path geometry on a page into a single tibble — pdf_extract_paths
code: pdf_extract_paths(path, page = 1L)

title: Extract all path geometry on a page into a single tibble — pdf_extract_paths
text: path Either character scalar path PDF file, already-open pdfium_doc returned pdf_open(). path character path document opened closed internally. page One-based page index (default 1).

title: Extract all path geometry on a page into a single tibble — pdf_extract_paths
text: tibble schema described .

title: Extract all path geometry on a page into a single tibble — pdf_extract_paths
text: row describes one path-segment operator (moveto, lineto, bezierto), order PDFium emits : Path identity & segment geometry: path_index - 1-based index parent path object page segment_index - 1-based segment index within path type - "moveto", "lineto", "bezierto", "unknown" x, y - segment's anchor / endpoint PDF points close - logical, segment closes current subpath Style (constant across rows one path): stroke_red, stroke_green, stroke_blue, stroke_alpha - 0-255 channels; NA stroke stroke_width - PDF points; NA stroke fill_red, fill_green, fill_blue, fill_alpha - 0-255 channels; NA fill Path bounding box (constant across rows one path): bounds_left, bounds_bottom, bounds_right, bounds_top - PDF points

title: Extract all path geometry on a page into a single tibble — pdf_extract_paths
text: page_size_pt - named numeric c(width, height) page, pdf_page_size() page_rotation - integer {0, 90, 180, 270}, pdf_page_rotation() text_runs - tibble one row per text object page: text_index, bounds, font_size, text (UTF-8 content extracted via pdf_text_content()).

title: Extract all path geometry on a page into a single tibble — pdf_extract_paths
text: Bezier control points exposed - segment endpoints. PDFium expose public C API; see dev/decisions/ADR-009-defer-bezier-controls.md.

title: Extract all path geometry on a page into a single tibble — pdf_extract_paths
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   paths <- pdf_extract_paths(fixture, page = 1)   head(paths)   attr(paths, "page_size_pt")   attr(paths, "text_runs") } #> # A tibble: 1 × 13 #>   text_index bounds_left bounds_bottom bounds_right bounds_top font_size text  #>        <int>       <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl> <chr> #> 1          5        129.          103.         159.       114.         1 Hello #> # ℹ 6 more variables: font_base_name <chr>, font_family <chr>, #> #   font_weight <int>, font_italic_angle <int>, font_is_embedded <lgl>, #> #   font_flags <int>

what: Load a single page from an open PDF document — pdf_load_page
title: Load a single page from an open PDF document — pdf_load_page
text: Returns pdfium_page handle bound parent doc. page garbage-collected finalizer calls FPDF_ClosePage; call pdf_close_page() explicitly need deterministic release. page keeps parent document alive long page reachable, safe drop reference doc still holding page.

title: Load a single page from an open PDF document — pdf_load_page
code: pdf_load_page(doc, page = 1L)

title: Load a single page from an open PDF document — pdf_load_page
text: doc pdfium_doc pdf_open(). page One-based page index. Must satisfy 1 <= page <= pdf_page_count(doc).

title: Load a single page from an open PDF document — pdf_load_page
text: pdfium_page object.

title: Load a single page from an open PDF document — pdf_load_page
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "minimal.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   page <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   pdf_close_page(page)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Axis-aligned bounding box of a page object — pdf_obj_bounds
title: Axis-aligned bounding box of a page object — pdf_obj_bounds
text: Returns smallest rectangle, PDF point coordinates, contains visible parts obj. bounds page's coordinate system, .e. origin bottom-left un-rotated media box (matching pdf_page_size()). Note bounds adjusted page's rotation; consult pdf_page_rotation() comparing positions across rotated pages.

title: Axis-aligned bounding box of a page object — pdf_obj_bounds
code: pdf_obj_bounds(obj)

title: Axis-aligned bounding box of a page object — pdf_obj_bounds
text: obj pdfium_obj pdf_page_objects().

title: Axis-aligned bounding box of a page object — pdf_obj_bounds
text: named numeric vector elements left, bottom, right, top. Width right - left, height top - bottom.

title: Axis-aligned bounding box of a page object — pdf_obj_bounds
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   objs <- pdf_page_objects(p)   pdf_obj_bounds(objs[[1]])   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Transformation matrix of a page object — pdf_obj_matrix
title: Transformation matrix of a page object — pdf_obj_matrix
text: Returns 2D affine transformation matrix attached obj, length-6 named numeric vector c(, b, c, d, e, f). matrix follows PDF convention: point (x, y) object's local space maps page-space coordinates (*x + c*y + e, b*x + d*y + f). paths drawn directly page matrix usually identity; text objects typically carry non-trivial matrix (Cairo example places text font-size 1 uses matrix scale position glyphs).

title: Transformation matrix of a page object — pdf_obj_matrix
code: pdf_obj_matrix(obj)

title: Transformation matrix of a page object — pdf_obj_matrix
text: obj pdfium_obj pdf_page_objects() (type).

title: Transformation matrix of a page object — pdf_obj_matrix
text: named numeric vector elements , b, c, d, e, f.

title: Transformation matrix of a page object — pdf_obj_matrix
text: Composing 3x3 matrix:
code: m <- pdf_obj_matrix(obj) mat <- matrix(c(m["a"], m["b"], 0,                 m["c"], m["d"], 0,                 m["e"], m["f"], 1),               nrow = 3, byrow = FALSE)

title: Transformation matrix of a page object — pdf_obj_matrix
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   pdf_obj_matrix(pdf_page_objects(p)[[1]])   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

text: Report type page object

code: pdf_obj_type(obj)

title: Report the type of a page object — pdf_obj_type
text: Character scalar: one "path", "text", "image", "form", "shading", "unknown".

title: Open a PDF document — pdf_open
text: Loads PDF file disk. returned pdfium_doc carries external pointer PDFium FPDF_DOCUMENT handle along finalizer calls FPDF_CloseDocument() R object garbage-collected. Call pdf_close() explicitly need deterministic release.

title: Open a PDF document — pdf_open
code: pdf_open(path, password = NULL)

title: Open a PDF document — pdf_open
text: path Character scalar. Path PDF file. file must exist readable. password Optional password encrypted PDFs. NULL (default) passes password PDFium, works unencrypted documents rare case empty-string-password encryption. Provide string document requires . Future minor releases broaden support password-protected PDFs; parameter present v0.1.0 reserve signature.

title: Open a PDF document — pdf_open
text: pdfium_doc object.

title: Open a PDF document — pdf_open
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "minimal.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   pdf_page_count(doc)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Count pages in a PDF document — pdf_page_count
title: Count pages in a PDF document — pdf_page_count
text: Returns number pages doc. Accepts either open pdfium_doc character path (case opens closes document internally — convenient one-shot inspection).

title: Count pages in a PDF document — pdf_page_count
code: pdf_page_count(doc)

title: Count pages in a PDF document — pdf_page_count
text: doc pdfium_doc pdf_open(), character scalar path.

title: Count pages in a PDF document — pdf_page_count
text: integer scalar — page count.

title: Count pages in a PDF document — pdf_page_count
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "minimal.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   pdf_page_count(fixture) } #> [1] 1

what: Enumerate the objects on a page — pdf_page_objects
title: Enumerate the objects on a page — pdf_page_objects
text: Returns list pdfium_obj handles - one per drawing primitive page, PDFium's z-order (back front). element carries type ("path", "text", "image", "form", "shading", "unknown"), 1-based index within page, internal pointer suitable passing downstream object queries.

title: Enumerate the objects on a page — pdf_page_objects
code: pdf_page_objects(page, page_num = 1L)

title: Enumerate the objects on a page — pdf_page_objects
text: page pdfium_page pdf_load_page(), pdfium_doc (case first page loaded closed automatically). page_num One-based page index. used page pdfium_doc. Ignored otherwise.

title: Enumerate the objects on a page — pdf_page_objects
text: list (possibly empty) pdfium_obj objects.

title: Enumerate the objects on a page — pdf_page_objects
text: Page objects lifetime - remain valid long parent pdfium_page open. handle's internal parent reference keeps page (transitively document) alive long hold object, calling pdf_close_page() explicitly invalidates returned objects.

title: Enumerate the objects on a page — pdf_page_objects
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   objs <- pdf_page_objects(p)   length(objs)   vapply(objs, pdf_obj_type, character(1))   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

title: Page rotation in degrees — pdf_page_rotation
text: Returns page's rotation 0, 90, 180, 270 degrees. PDFium reports rotation stored page's /Rotate entry combined runtime rotation applied via editing API.

title: Page rotation in degrees — pdf_page_rotation
code: pdf_page_rotation(x, page = 1L)

title: Page rotation in degrees — pdf_page_rotation
text: x pdfium_page pdf_load_page(), pdfium_doc. page One-based page index. used x pdfium_doc. Ignored otherwise.

title: Page rotation in degrees — pdf_page_rotation
Page rotation in degrees — pdf_page_rotation
text: non-zero rotation means pdf_page_size()'s width height refer page's pre-rotation media box, -screen dimensions viewer display. "-displayed" size, swap width height rotation 90 270.

title: Page rotation in degrees — pdf_page_rotation
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "minimal.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   pdf_page_rotation(doc, 1)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Page dimensions in PDF points — pdf_page_size
title: Page dimensions in PDF points — pdf_page_size
text: Returns width height page PDF points (1 point = 1/72 inch). Accepts either pdfium_page (preferred already one) (doc, page) pair (convenience one-shot inspection).

title: Page dimensions in PDF points — pdf_page_size
code: pdf_page_size(x, page = 1L)

title: Page dimensions in PDF points — pdf_page_size
text: x pdfium_page pdf_load_page(), pdfium_doc. page One-based page index. used x pdfium_doc. Ignored otherwise.

title: Page dimensions in PDF points — pdf_page_size
text: named numeric vector elements width height.

title: Page dimensions in PDF points — pdf_page_size
text: returned dimensions media-box dimensions page's default (un-rotated) orientation. page non-zero rotation (via PDF /Rotate attribute PDFium's runtime rotation), pdf_page_size() swap width height. Query rotation separately pdf_page_rotation() need know -screen orientation.

title: Page dimensions in PDF points — pdf_page_size
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "minimal.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   pdf_page_size(doc, 1)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Dash pattern of a path page-object — pdf_path_dash
title: Dash pattern of a path page-object — pdf_path_dash
text: Returns dash array (PDF points) dash phase (offset pattern, points) attached obj's stroke. solid (un-dashed) path returns empty array phase 0.

title: Dash pattern of a path page-object — pdf_path_dash
code: pdf_path_dash(obj)

title: Dash pattern of a path page-object — pdf_path_dash
text: obj pdfium_obj type "path" (pdf_page_objects()).

title: Dash pattern of a path page-object — pdf_path_dash
text: named list two elements: array - numeric vector dash lengths PDF points; length-zero solid lines. phase - numeric scalar, dash-pattern phase points (typically 0).

title: Dash pattern of a path page-object — pdf_path_dash
text: dash array c(3, 2) example means: draw 3 points, skip 2 points, repeat. phase shifts pattern first segment starts.

title: Dash pattern of a path page-object — pdf_path_dash
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   path_obj <- Filter(\(o) o$type == "path", pdf_page_objects(p))[[1]]   pdf_path_dash(path_obj)   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Fill color of a path page-object — pdf_path_fill
title: Fill color of a path page-object — pdf_path_fill
text: Returns RGBA fill color obj. Channels integers [0, 255]. PDFium reports object fill set (e.g. stroke-path), four channels NA.

title: Fill color of a path page-object — pdf_path_fill
code: pdf_path_fill(obj)

title: Fill color of a path page-object — pdf_path_fill
text: obj pdfium_obj type "path" (pdf_page_objects()).

title: Fill color of a path page-object — pdf_path_fill
text: named numeric vector c(red, green, blue, alpha) 0-255 channel values, -NA fill set.

title: Fill color of a path page-object — pdf_path_fill
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   path_obj <- Filter(\(o) o$type == "path", pdf_page_objects(p))[[1]]   pdf_path_fill(path_obj)   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Path segments of a path page-object — pdf_path_segments
title: Path segments of a path page-object — pdf_path_segments
text: Returns one row per segment path. Segments emitted order appear page's content stream, order PDFium's rendering pipeline consumes. result suitable plotting geometry downstream coordinate analysis.

title: Path segments of a path page-object — pdf_path_segments
code: pdf_path_segments(obj)

title: Path segments of a path page-object — pdf_path_segments
text: obj pdfium_obj type "path" (pdf_page_objects()).

title: Path segments of a path page-object — pdf_path_segments
text: tibble columns described . empty path returns 0-row tibble shape.

title: Path segments of a path page-object — pdf_path_segments
text: row carries: index - 1-based segment index within path type - "moveto", "lineto", "bezierto", "unknown" x, y - segment's anchor point PDF points close - TRUE segment closes current subpath (PDFium's h operator equivalent) Known limitation: PDFium's segment readout API exposes endpoint bezierto segment, two control points. public C API offers way recover ; limitation shared pypdfium2, pdfium-rs, pdfium-render. now, bezierto rows show curve's endpoint; control-point information lost. See dev/decisions/ADR-009-defer-bezier-controls.md decision record.

title: Path segments of a path page-object — pdf_path_segments
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   path_obj <- Filter(\(o) o$type == "path", pdf_page_objects(p))[[1]]   pdf_path_segments(path_obj)   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Stroke style of a path page-object — pdf_path_stroke
title: Stroke style of a path page-object — pdf_path_stroke
text: Returns RGBA stroke color stroke width obj. Color channels integers [0, 255]; width PDF points. PDFium reports object stroke set, color channels NA width NA.

title: Stroke style of a path page-object — pdf_path_stroke
code: pdf_path_stroke(obj)

title: Stroke style of a path page-object — pdf_path_stroke
text: obj pdfium_obj type "path" (pdf_page_objects()).

title: Stroke style of a path page-object — pdf_path_stroke
text: named list two elements: color - named numeric vector c(red, green, blue, alpha) 0-255 channel values, -NA stroke set. width - stroke width PDF points, NA stroke set.

title: Stroke style of a path page-object — pdf_path_stroke
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   path_obj <- Filter(\(o) o$type == "path", pdf_page_objects(p))[[1]]   pdf_path_stroke(path_obj)   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Text content of a text page-object — pdf_text_content
title: Text content of a text page-object — pdf_text_content
text: Returns Unicode text obj single character string. PDFium produces UTF-16LE internally; wrapper converts UTF-8 encoding flag set R prints non-ASCII glyphs correctly.

title: Text content of a text page-object — pdf_text_content
code: pdf_text_content(obj)

title: Text content of a text page-object — pdf_text_content
text: obj pdfium_obj type "text" (pdf_page_objects()).

title: Text content of a text page-object — pdf_text_content
text: character scalar (UTF-8 encoded). empty text object returns "".

title: Text content of a text page-object — pdf_text_content
text: Loading text PDF requires per-page text-extraction context (FPDFText_LoadPage / FPDFText_ClosePage). wrapper opens closes context internally every call. need many text objects one page, upcoming pdf_text_runs() (Phase 3 slice 2) share single text-page across entire page avoid per-call overhead.

title: Text content of a text page-object — pdf_text_content
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   text_obj <- Filter(\(o) o$type == "text", pdf_page_objects(p))[[1]]   pdf_text_content(text_obj)   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Font metadata of a text page-object — pdf_text_font
title: Font metadata of a text page-object — pdf_text_font
text: Returns font properties PDFium exposes obj's text: base font name (e.g. "Helvetica-Bold"), family name (e.g. "Helvetica"), weight (typographic weight integer, 400 = regular, 700 = bold), italic angle degrees (negative italic slant), whether font embedded PDF, PDF font-descriptor flags bitmask (see PDF spec section "Font Descriptors", Table 123).

title: Font metadata of a text page-object — pdf_text_font
code: pdf_text_font(obj)

title: Font metadata of a text page-object — pdf_text_font
text: obj pdfium_obj type "text" (pdf_page_objects()).

title: Font metadata of a text page-object — pdf_text_font
text: named list elements: base_name - character scalar, base font name; UTF-8 family - character scalar, font family name; UTF-8 weight - integer (e.g. 400, 500, 700) italic_angle - integer degrees; 0 upright is_embedded - logical flags - integer bitmask

title: Font metadata of a text page-object — pdf_text_font
text: text object font set (rare; usually malformed PDFs), every field NA.

title: Font metadata of a text page-object — pdf_text_font
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   text_obj <- Filter(\(o) o$type == "text", pdf_page_objects(p))[[1]]   pdf_text_font(text_obj)   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Font size of a text page-object — pdf_text_font_size
title: Font size of a text page-object — pdf_text_font_size
text: Returns typographic ("em") font size, PDF points, set text object. raw size stored PDF; scaled object's transformation matrix. PDF producers often emit text em-size 1 let CTM scaling (Cairo's PDF backend works way). recover -page rendered size, multiply value y-scale object's matrix (matrix accessor lands later phase).

title: Font size of a text page-object — pdf_text_font_size
code: pdf_text_font_size(obj)

title: Font size of a text page-object — pdf_text_font_size
text: obj pdfium_obj type "text" (pdf_page_objects()).

title: Font size of a text page-object — pdf_text_font_size
text: Numeric scalar PDF points, NA_real_ PDFium reports font size (rare; usually malformed PDFs).

title: Font size of a text page-object — pdf_text_font_size
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "shapes.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   p <- pdf_load_page(doc, 1)   text_obj <- Filter(\(o) o$type == "text", pdf_page_objects(p))[[1]]   pdf_text_font_size(text_obj)   pdf_close_page(p)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: Extract every text run on a page — pdf_text_runs
title: Extract every text run on a page — pdf_text_runs
text: Returns one row per text page-object page, text content, bounding box, font size, 1-based page-object index. Loads PDFium's per-page text-extraction context (FPDFText_LoadPage) reuses across every text object page; materially faster calling pdf_text_content() loop, opens closes text page per object.

title: Extract every text run on a page — pdf_text_runs
code: pdf_text_runs(page, page_num = 1L)

title: Extract every text run on a page — pdf_text_runs
text: page pdfium_page pdf_load_page(), pdfium_doc (case first page loaded closed automatically). page_num One-based page index. used page pdfium_doc. Ignored otherwise.

title: Extract every text run on a page — pdf_text_runs
text: tibble columns: text_index - 1-based page-object index (row text_index-th object returned pdf_page_objects()) bounds_left, bounds_bottom, bounds_right, bounds_top object's bounding box PDF points font_size - typographic em size; multiply text object's matrix scale (available) rendered size text - UTF-8 string

title: Extract every text run on a page — pdf_text_runs
text: returned tibble's schema matches text_runs attribute produced pdf_extract_paths().

title: Extract every text run on a page — pdf_text_runs
code: fixture <- system.file("extdata", "fixtures", "unicode.pdf",                        package = "pdfium") if (nzchar(fixture)) {   doc <- pdf_open(fixture)   pdf_text_runs(doc, 1)   pdf_close(doc) }

what: pdfium: Idiomatic R Bindings to the PDFium PDF Engine — pdfium-package
title: pdfium: Idiomatic R Bindings to the PDFium PDF Engine — pdfium-package
text: Read PDF documents level pages, page objects, path geometry using Google's PDFium engine. Surfaces path segments, stroke fill style, transformation matrices, text positions content, font metadata, image metadata, page rendering.

title: pdfium: Idiomatic R Bindings to the PDFium PDF Engine — pdfium-package
text: Open document pdf_open() inspect basic facts pdf_page_count(). Higher-level helpers (path extraction, text runs, rendering) arrive subsequent releases.

title: pdfium: Idiomatic R Bindings to the PDFium PDF Engine — pdfium-package
text: underlying libpdfium shared library downloaded bblanchon/pdfium-binaries first time package installed. pinned version lives tools/pdfium-version.txt.

title: pdfium: Idiomatic R Bindings to the PDFium PDF Engine — pdfium-package
text: Maintainer: Bill Denney wdenney@humanpredictions.com (ORCID) Authors: Bill Denney wdenney@humanpredictions.com (ORCID) contributors: PDFium Authors (Authors bundled PDFium binaries (BSD-3-Clause)) [copyright holder]

title: pdfium 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial scaffolding: R package layout, Rcpp toolchain, GitHub CI matrix, pre-commit configuration, decision records, architecture documentation. Binary distribution: PDFium downloaded bblanchon/pdfium-binaries install time. See dev/decisions/ADR-003-binary-distribution.md. Smoke test: pdf_page_count(path) proves end--end Rcpp toolchain fixture PDF. release yet feature complete. See project plan roadmap v0.1.0.
